name: Chris Koster
subject: Happy Independence Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Koster for Missouri, Gary Mallory, Treasurer |
body:
Hi,

Before you step out to attend a parade or watch some fireworks, I'd like to wish you and your family a very happy Independence Day.

Today is about celebrating the individuals who have sacrificed to make this country a better place, including the men and women serving our country in America’s Armed Forces.

240 years ago, our Founding Fathers declared that we are all created equal and are entitled to life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness. While we join together today with friends and family to celebrate these American values and everything that makes our country great, the members of our Armed Forces continue to fight for these rights at home and overseas.

Please join with me today to honor those who continue to fight to protect our freedoms, and keep us safe here -- and across the world.

Have a safe and happy holiday,

Chris

------------------------------------------------------------------
| Paid for by Koster for Missouri, Gary Mallory, Treasurer |
------------------------------------------------------------------

Koster for Missouri
PO Box 1551
Jefferson City MO 65102 United States

If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please https://act.myngp.com/el/4907066075398932480/7315393408091424768.

name: John Gregg
subject: Happy Independence Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gregg for Indiana
body:
I'll be celebrating by thinking about the values our forefathers fought for, the freedoms that we celebrate -- and how we must remain vigilant to protect those freedoms today.
| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/813859163095828480/-5366743142583891968?utm_campaign=engg_july4_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&refcode=em16_160704_engg_gq_july4_ful__jg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)
---
Peter, This Independence Day, I'm looking forward to spending time with family and friends, marching in parades with my running mate Christina Hale, and watching some fireworks. But I'll also be celebrating by thinking about the values our forefathers fought for, the freedoms that we celebrate -- and how we must remain ever-vigilant in protecting them for us and future generations. As your next governor, I'll keep on fighting to protect the freedoms and civil rights of every Hoosier, no matter who they are, what they look like, or who they love. And, I'll work towards increasing educational and economic opportunities that help Hoosiers achieve their full potential. But today, I hope you have the chance to celebrate with the people you love. Happy Independence Day, John Gregg
| | [**Contribute ->**](https://act.myngp.com/el/813859163095828480/-5366743142583891968?utm_campaign=engg_july4_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&refcode=em16_160704_engg_gq_july4_ful__jg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)

name: Bob Fertik, Democrats.com
subject: Join VoteVets: Stop the privatization of veterans health care
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS ACTION FUND
body:
| | | [](http://mail.democrats.com/CT00128801MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2016-07-04)
---|---|---
Dear Activist, There are over 8 million veterans enrolled to receive a wide range of services through the VA. In 2013 alone, the VA accommodated almost 90 million outpatient visits; that's an average of almost 250,000 appointments every day.

Those numbers should help explain why for-profit hospitals and people like the Koch Brothers are fighting so hard to privatize veterans' health care.

I served two tours in Kuwait during Operation Iraqi Freedom, and I get great care from the VA. And while we should always strive to decrease wait times, I am not alone. According to independent polls, over 90% of veterans approve of their inpatient and outpatient care through the VA.

In 2014 after the VA crisis, Congress passed a large veterans health care bill that authorized funds to hire more doctors and nurses to keep up with the influx of veterans recently returned from two new wars overseas. But the legislation also called for a commission to develop recommendations for improving veterans' health care (The Commission on Care). And I am sad to tell you, a number of members of the commission are trying to phase out all VA hospitals and clinics over the next two decades.

We can stop them.

[**Sign our petition calling on the "Commission on Care" to stop trying to privatize veterans health care and instead focus on improving access to a well-funded, properly staffed VA**](http://mail.democrats.com/CT00128802MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2016-07-04).

Privatizing the system would severely under-fund the department, leaving many veterans with service-connected injuries out in the cold. Often times, VA centers are equipped to deal with specialized care, amputations and traumatic brain injuries, in a way a local doctor or hospital may not be.

For veterans in need of specialized care, both physical and mental, they may not have a caregiver to turn to in their area.

But more to the point, as a RAND study recently indicated, "the quality of care provided by the VA health system generally was as good as or better than other health systems on most quality measures."

[**Sign our petition calling on the "Commission on Care" to stop trying to privatize veterans health care and instead focus on improving access to a well-funded, properly staffed VA**](http://mail.democrats.com/CT00128803MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2016-07-04).

This is not only a very real legislative issue, but it will also be an election issue. The Koch Brothers, who have an employee on the "Commission on Care," are going to spend untold millions on the presidential and senate races through their shell organization, Concerned Veterans for America.

That is why it is so important we stand together to make our voices heard.
All my best, Rich Hegdahl
Iraq War Veteran
VoteVets

**[Add Your Name](http://mail.democrats.com/CT00128804MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2016-07-04 "http://action.votevets.org/page/s/stop-privatization?source=em160503&utm_medium=email&utm_source=votevets&utm_campaign=va-privatization&utm_content=commission-on-care")**

PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS ACTION FUND
* * *
Democrats.com is the oldest online community of progressive activists, with over 2 million
supporters. We fight for jobs, justice, healthcare, education, the environment, and peace.
We're supported by great progressive partners so we never ask for donations.
Please join us at **[Democrats.com](http://democrats.com)**! You are subscriber #10036727 to Democrats.com Activist Alerts.
[Click here](http://archive.democrats.com/unsub.cfm?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&fieldname=L45) if you do not want future messages from VoteVets.org
If you do not want messages from any of our partners [click here](http://archive.democrats.com/unsub.cfm?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&fieldname=noads).
[Click here](http://archive.democrats.com/unsub.cfm?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&fieldname=L1) to permanently unsubscribe from all Democrats.com lists.

name: Roy Cooper
subject: Happy July 4th
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
Independence Day provides an opportunity to relax with loved ones and reflect on the history of our great nation.
|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12021f7/2fde7c66/40112c74/156a01c3/1197284207/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cooper-jun16eoq?utm_campaign=fnd_eoq5_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&refcode=em16_160629_fnd_gq_eoq5_ful__rc&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_content=1")
---
Friend, Independence Day provides an opportunity to relax with loved ones and reflect on the history of our great nation. On this day, we rejoice in our freedom and memorialize those who work so hard to protect it. We owe so much to our armed service members, veterans and military families who have made sacrifices to defend our country. They are the reason that we are able to celebrate today. From the Blue Ridge Mountains to the Outer Banks, I hope that you have a happy and safe Fourth of July and that you join me in taking time to consider all of the blessings that come along with being a North Carolinian and an American. From my family to yours, Roy

* * *

Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
---

Roy Cooper for North Carolina
PO Box 10587
Raleigh NC 27605 United States

This email was sent to:
dpwillis67@gmail.com

To unsubscribe, go to:
[http://act.roycooper.com/unsubscribe](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12021f7/2fde7c66/40112c74/156a01c2/1197284207/VEsE/ "http://act.roycooper.com/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=2+-+httpactroycoopercomunsubscribe&utm_campaign=em16_160704_fnd_gq_july4_ful__rc&source=em16_160704_fnd_gq_july4_ful__rc")

name: Bill Cole
subject: Have a Safe July 4th
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF COLE
body:
| |
---
| Friend --
On the heels of some of the worst flooding to hit our state, please continue to pray for the twenty-three families who lost loved ones in last month’s flooding. With more storms and rain hitting regions of our state, I encourage everyone to stay safe and be vigilant for flooding that can occur quickly. I also ask all West Virginians to continue to help their neighbors by making sure they are safe. As I have traveled the state, it has been heartwarming to me to see people who lost everything have gratitude to have survived and resolve to rebuild. It has also been amazing to witness the outpouring of support from fellow West Virginians. July 4th is a uniquely American holiday where we take time to commemorate the birth of our nation, celebrate the freedoms we as Americans enjoy, and honor the ultimate sacrifice so many men and women have given to our country. I stand side by side with my fellow West Virginians and will continue to do all I can on our road to recovery. We are a caring people – proud and strong – and together we will rebuild.
I will spend my day traveling to the already flood impacted areas in Kanawha and Clay counties to bring more supplies and support. I wish each of you a safe July 4th. May God bless the great state of West Virginia, her people and the United States of America.

[http://www.billcolewv.com/](http://www.billcolewv.com/?e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=july_4th&n=1)

This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](dpwillis67@gmail.com). To stop receiving emails, [click here](http://www.billcolewv.com/unsubscribe?e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=july_4th&n=8).
Created with [NationBuilder](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fnationbuilder.com&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=july_4th&n=9), the essential toolkit for leaders.
clean_body: | | | Friend -- On the heels of some of the worst flooding to hit our state, please continue to pray for the twenty-three families who lost loved ones in last month’s flooding. With more storms and rain hitting regions of our state, I encourage everyone to stay safe and be vigilant for flooding that can occur quickly. I also ask all West Virginians to continue to help their neighbors by making sure they are safe. As I have traveled the state, it has been heartwarming to me to see people who lost everything have gratitude to have survived and resolve to rebuild. It has also been amazing to witness the outpouring of support from fellow West Virginians. July 4th is a uniquely American holiday where we take time to commemorate the birth of our nation, celebrate the freedoms we as Americans enjoy, and honor the ultimate sacrifice so many men and women have given to our country. I stand side by side with my fellow West Virginians and will continue to do all I can on our road to recovery. We are a caring people – proud and strong – and together we will rebuild. I will spend my day traveling to the already flood impacted areas in Kanawha and Clay counties to bring more supplies and support. I wish each of you a safe July 4th. May God bless the great state of West Virginia, her people and the United States of America. | | | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- | PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF COLE Friends of Bill Cole · United States

name: Carly Fiorina
subject: Happy Independence Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CARLY FOR AMERICA
body:
| | | | On this day, 240 years ago, our Founders signed a sacred document.
---
---
| | | [](http://carlyforamerica.msgfocus.com/c/114BzLvBEt6qUjrdk132YlW)
---
| | |
## Friend,
---
| On this day, 240 years ago, our Founders signed their names to a sacred document. They chose a war for freedom over a peace with tyranny. And they fought for an idea--the idea of a nation built on the radical notion that every citizen would have the right to fulfill their God-given potential. The nation they built, our nation, has endured. Our experiment of self government has survived war and depression. It has produced statesmen of courage and sacrifice. And it has taught the world that a government by the people, of the people, and for the people can build the strongest nation on earth. Today, we are increasingly faced with threats to our freedoms, our sovereignty, and our way of life. And, just as our ancestors stood and fought for the character of our nation, we the people must fight for the future of our country with the same courage, conviction, and optimism. Because, even after 240 years, this is what we as Americans know: our nation is blessed, our nation is exceptional, and our nation is worth fighting for. Happy Independence Day. **Carly**

---
---

| | |
##### PAID FOR BY CARLY FOR AMERICA
